min_audio_length.description: The minimum length of an audio sample in seconds. Audio shorter than this will be ignored during preprocessing.
min_audio_length.title: Min Audio Length

max_audio_length.description: The maximum length of an audio sample in seconds. Audio longer than this will be ignored during preprocessing. Increasing the max_audio_length will result in larger memory usage. If you are running out of memory, consider lowering the max_audio_length.
max_audio_length.title: Max Audio Length

max_wav_value.description: Advanced. The maximum value allowed to be in your wav files. For 16-bit audio, this should be (2**16)/2 - 1.
max_wav_value.title: Max Wav Value

input_sampling_rate.description: The sampling rate describes the number of samples per second of audio. The 'input_sampling_rate' is with respect to your vocoder, or spec-to-wav model. This means that the spectrograms predicted by your text-to-spec model will also be calculated from audio at this sampling rate. If you change this value, your audio will automatically be re-sampled during preprocessing.
input_sampling_rate.title: Input Sampling Rate

output_sampling_rate.description: Advanced. The sampling rate describes the number of samples per second of audio. The 'output_sampling_rate' is with respect to your vocoder, or spec-to-wav model. This means that the wav files generated by your vocoder or spec-to-wav model will be at this sampling rate. If you change this value, you will also need to change the upsample rates in your vocoder. Your audio will automatically be re-sampled during preprocessing.
output_sampling_rate.title: Output Sampling Rate

alignment_sampling_rate.description: Advanced. The sampling rate describes the number of samples per second of audio. The 'alignment_sampling_rate' describes the sampling rate used when training an alignment model. If you change this value, your audio will automatically be re-sampled during preprocessing.
alignment_sampling_rate.title: Alignment Sampling Rate

target_bit_depth.description: Advanced. This is the bit depth of each sample in your audio files.
target_bit_depth.title: Target Bit Depth

n_fft.description: Advanced. This is the number of bins used by the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT).
n_fft.title: FFT Size

fft_window_size.description: Advanced. This is the window size used by the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT).
fft_window_size.title: FFT Window Size

fft_hop_size.description: Advanced. This is the hop size for calculating the Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) which calculates a sequence of spectrograms from a single audio file. Another way of putting it is that the hop size is equal to the amount of non-intersecting samples from the audio in each spectrogram.
fft_hop_size.title: FFT Hop Size

f_min.description: Advanced. This is the minimum frequency for the lowest frequency bin when calculating the spectrogram.
f_min.title: Minimum Frequency

f_max.description: Advanced. This is the maximum frequency for the highest frequency bin when calculating the spectrogram.
f_max.title: Maximum Frequency

n_mels.description: Advanced. This is the number of filters in the Mel-scale spaced filterbank.
n_mels.title: Number of Mel bins

spec_type.description: Advanced. Defines how to calculate the spectrogram. 'mel' uses the TorchAudio implementation for a Mel spectrogram. 'mel-librosa' uses Librosa's implementation. 'linear' calculates a non-Mel linear spectrogram and 'raw' calculates a complex-valued spectrogram. 'linear' and 'raw' are not currently supported by EveryVoice. We recommend using 'mel-librosa'.
spec_type.title: Spec Type

vocoder_segment_size.description: Advanced. The vocoder, or spec-to-wav model is trained by sampling random fixed-size sections of the audio. This value specifies the number of samples in those sections.
vocoder_segment_size.title: Vocoder Segment Size

contact_name.description: The name of the contact person or organization responsible for answering questions related to this model.
contact_name.title: Contact Name

contact_email.description: The email address of the contact person or organization responsible for answering questions related to this model.
contact_email.title: Contact Email

label.description: A label for the source of data
label.title: Label

permissions_obtained.description: An attestation that permission has been obtained to use this data. You may not use EveryVoice to build a TTS system with data that you do not have permission to use and there are serious possible consequences for doing so. Finding data online does not constitute permission. The speaker should be aware and consent to their data being used in this way.
permissions_obtained.title: Permissions Obtained

data_dir.description: The path to the directory with your audio files.
data_dir.title: Data Dir

filelist.description: The path to your dataset's filelist.
filelist.title: Filelist

filelist_loader.description: Advanced. The file-loader function to use to load your dataset's filelist.
filelist_loader.title: Filelist Loader

sox_effects.description: Advanced. A list of SoX effects to apply to your audio prior to preprocessing. Run python -c 'import torchaudio; print(torchaudio.sox_effects.effect_names())' to see a list of supported effects.
sox_effects.title: Sox Effects

strong.description: All characters that constitute strong boundaries, for one language.
strong.title: Strong

weak.description: All characters that constitute strong boundaries, for one language.
weak.title: Weak

LoggerConfig.description:
The logger configures all the information needed for where to store your experiment's logs and checkpoints.
The structure of your logs will then be:
<name> / <version> / <sub_dir>
<sub_dir> will be generated by calling <sub_dir_callable> each time the LoggerConfig is constructed.

name.description: The name of the experiment. The structure of your logs will be <name> / <version> / <sub_dir>.
name.title: Experiment Name

save_dir.description: The directory to save your checkpoints and logs to.
save_dir.title: Save Dir

sub_dir_callable.description: The function that generates a string to call your runs - by default this is a timestamp. The structure of your logs will be <name> / <version> / <sub_dir> where <sub_dir> is a timestamp.
sub_dir_callable.title: Sub Dir Callable

version.description: The version of your experiment. The structure of your logs will be <name> / <version> / <sub_dir>.
version.title: Version

LoggerConfig.title: LoggerConfig

train_split.description: The amount of the dataset to use for training. The rest will be used as validation. Hold some of the validation set out for a test set if you are performing experiments.
train_split.title: Train Split

dataset_split_seed.description: The seed to use when splitting the dataset into train and validation sets.
dataset_split_seed.title: Dataset Split Seed

save_dir.description: The directory to save preprocessed files to.
save_dir.title: Save Dir

path_to_audio_config_file.description: The path to an audio configuration file.
path_to_audio_config_file.title: Path To Audio Config File

exclamations.description: Exclamation punctuation symbols used in your datasets. Replaces these symbols with <EXCL> internally.
exclamations.title: Exclamations

question_symbols.description: Question/interrogative punctuation symbols used in your datasets. Replaces these symbols with <QINT> internally.
question_symbols.title: Question Symbols

quotemarks.description: Quotemark punctuation symbols used in your datasets. Replaces these symbols with <QUOTE> internally.
quotemarks.title: Quotemarks

parentheses.description: Punctuation symbols indicating parentheses, brackets, or braces. Replaces these symbols with <PAREN> internally.
parentheses.title: Parentheses

periods.description: Punctuation symbols indicating a 'period' used in your datasets. Replaces these symbols with <PERIOD> internally.
periods.title: Periods

colons.description: Punctuation symbols indicating a 'colon' used in your datasets. Replaces these symbols with <COLON> internally.
colons.title: Colons

semi_colons.description: Punctuation symbols indicating a 'semi-colon' used in your datasets. Replaces these symbols with <SEMICOL> internally.
semi_colons.title: Semi Colons

hyphens.description: Punctuation symbols indicating a 'hyphen' used in your datasets. * is a hyphen by default since unidecode decodes middle-dot punctuation as an asterisk. Replaces these symbols with <HYPHEN> internally.
hyphens.title: Hyphens

commas.description: Punctuation symbols indicating a 'comma' used in your datasets. Replaces these symbols with <COMMA> internally.
commas.title: Commas

ellipses.description: Punctuation symbols indicating ellipses used in your datasets. Replaces these symbols with <EPS> internally.
ellipses.title: Ellipses

StyleTTS2ASRConfig.description: Source for the ASR text-aligner checkpoint.

repo_id.description: HuggingFace repo ID for the ASR text-aligner.
repo_id.title: Repo Id

checkpoint_filename.description: Filename of the model checkpoint within the HuggingFace repo.
checkpoint_filename.title: Checkpoint Filename

config_filename.description: Filename of the model config within the HuggingFace repo.
config_filename.title: Config Filename

local_checkpoint.description: Local path to the checkpoint file. If set, overrides repo_id/checkpoint_filename. Warning: this path is expanded (via os.path.expandvars and os.path.expanduser) when the config is loaded, and the resolved absolute path is what gets embedded in saved checkpoints. This may make your checkpoints less portable to other machines. Environment variables (e.g. $HOME or ${MY_MODELS}) and '~' are supported.
local_checkpoint.title: Local Checkpoint

local_config.description: Local path to the config file. If set, overrides repo_id/config_filename. Warning: this path is expanded (via os.path.expandvars and os.path.expanduser) when the config is loaded, and the resolved absolute path is what gets embedded in saved checkpoints. This may make your checkpoints less portable to other machines. Environment variables (e.g. $HOME or ${MY_MODELS}) and '~' are supported.
local_config.title: Local Config

StyleTTS2ASRConfig.title: StyleTTS2ASRConfig

StyleTTS2DecoderConfig.description:
HiFiGAN/iSTFTNet decoder config for StyleTTS2.

Inherits all HiFiGAN fields. ``istft_layer=True`` selects iSTFTNet;
``istft_layer=False`` selects plain HiFiGAN. The extra iSTFT fields
are only used when ``istft_layer=True``.

Note: ``activation_function``, ``msd_layers``, and ``mpd_layers`` are
inherited but not forwarded to StyleTTS2 — discriminators are configured
separately inside the StyleTTS2 model builder.

resblock.description: Which resblock to use. See Kong et. al. 2020: https://arxiv.org/abs/2010.05646

upsample_initial_channel.description: The number of dimensions to project the Mel inputs to before being passed to the resblock.
upsample_initial_channel.title: Upsample Initial Channel

resblock_kernel_sizes.description: The kernel size of each convolutional layer in the resblock.
resblock_kernel_sizes.title: Resblock Kernel Sizes

resblock_dilation_sizes.description: The dilations of each convolution in each layer of the resblock.
resblock_dilation_sizes.title: Resblock Dilation Sizes

msd_layers.description: The number of layers to use in the Multi-Scale Discriminator.
msd_layers.title: Msd Layers

mpd_layers.description: The size of each layer in the Multi-Period Discriminator.
mpd_layers.title: Mpd Layers

gen_istft_n_fft.description: FFT size for the iSTFTNet generator. Only used when istft_layer=True.
gen_istft_n_fft.title: Gen Istft N Fft

gen_istft_hop_size.description: Hop size for the iSTFTNet generator. Only used when istft_layer=True.
gen_istft_hop_size.title: Gen Istft Hop Size

StyleTTS2DecoderConfig.title: StyleTTS2DecoderConfig

estimate_sigma_data.description: Estimate sigma_data from each batch instead of using the fixed value.
estimate_sigma_data.title: Estimate Sigma Data

StyleTTS2JDCConfig.description: Source for the JDC F0 extractor checkpoint.

repo_id.description: HuggingFace repo ID for the JDC F0 extractor.
repo_id.title: Repo Id

filename.description: Filename of the checkpoint within the HuggingFace repo.
filename.title: Filename

local_path.description: Local path to the checkpoint. If set, overrides repo_id/filename. Warning: this path is expanded (via os.path.expandvars and os.path.expanduser) when the config is loaded, and the resolved absolute path is what gets embedded in saved checkpoints. This may make your checkpoints less portable to other machines. Environment variables (e.g. $HOME or ${MY_MODELS}) and '~' are supported.
local_path.title: Local Path

StyleTTS2JDCConfig.title: StyleTTS2JDCConfig

tma_epoch.description: Epoch at which TMA training begins (stage 1).
tma_epoch.title: Tma Epoch

diff_epoch.description: Epoch at which style diffusion training begins (stage 2).
diff_epoch.title: Diff Epoch

joint_epoch.description: Epoch at which joint (decoder + style encoder) training begins (stage 2).
joint_epoch.title: Joint Epoch

StyleTTS2ModelConfig.description:
Architecture hyperparameters for StyleTTS2.

Note: ``n_mels`` and ``n_token`` are intentionally absent — they are
derived at runtime from ``preprocessing.audio.n_mels`` and
``len(TextProcessor(text).symbols)`` respectively in the translation layer.

target_text_representation_level.description: Whether to train on 'characters' or 'ipa_phones'. Must match the representation used during everyvoice preprocess.

max_dur.description: Maximum duration (frames) for a single phoneme.
max_dur.title: Max Dur

StyleTTS2ModelConfig.title: StyleTTS2ModelConfig

ft_lr.description: Learning rate for acoustic modules during stage 2 / fine-tuning.
ft_lr.title: Ft Lr

StyleTTS2PLBERTConfig.description: Source for the PLBERT text encoder checkpoint.

repo_id.description: HuggingFace repo ID for the PLBERT text encoder.
repo_id.title: Repo Id

checkpoint_filename.description: Filename of the checkpoint within the HuggingFace repo.
checkpoint_filename.title: Checkpoint Filename

config_filename.description: Filename of the model config within the HuggingFace repo.
config_filename.title: Config Filename

local_checkpoint.description: Local path to the checkpoint file. If set, overrides repo_id/checkpoint_filename. Warning: this path is expanded (via os.path.expandvars and os.path.expanduser) when the config is loaded, and the resolved absolute path is what gets embedded in saved checkpoints. This may make your checkpoints less portable to other machines. Environment variables (e.g. $HOME or ${MY_MODELS}) and '~' are supported.
local_checkpoint.title: Local Checkpoint

local_config.description: Local path to the config file. If set, overrides repo_id/config_filename. Warning: this path is expanded (via os.path.expandvars and os.path.expanduser) when the config is loaded, and the resolved absolute path is what gets embedded in saved checkpoints. This may make your checkpoints less portable to other machines. Environment variables (e.g. $HOME or ${MY_MODELS}) and '~' are supported.
local_config.title: Local Config

StyleTTS2PLBERTConfig.title: StyleTTS2PLBERTConfig

StyleTTS2PretrainedConfig.description: Sources for the frozen pretrained models used by StyleTTS2.

f0.description: JDC F0 extractor source (HuggingFace repo or local path).

asr.description: ASR text-aligner source (HuggingFace repo or local paths).

plbert.description: PLBERT text encoder source (HuggingFace repo or local paths).

pretrained_symbols.description: Ordered symbol list that matches the pretrained text-encoder embedding table. The index of each symbol here is its embedding-table row. Override only if you are using a custom pretrained text encoder.
pretrained_symbols.title: Pretrained Symbols

StyleTTS2PretrainedConfig.title: StyleTTS2PretrainedConfig

min_len.description: Minimum sample length for SLM adversarial loss.
min_len.title: Min Len

max_len.description: Maximum sample length for SLM adversarial loss.
max_len.title: Max Len

batch_percentage.description: Fraction of the batch to use for SLM adversarial loss (to save memory).
batch_percentage.title: Batch Percentage

iter.description: Update the SLM discriminator every this many generator steps.
iter.title: Iter

thresh.description: Gradient norm threshold above which gradients are scaled.
thresh.title: Thresh

scale.description: Gradient scaling factor applied to predictor/diffusion gradients from the SLM discriminator.
scale.title: Scale

model.description: HuggingFace model ID for the speech language model.
model.title: Model

sr.description: Sampling rate expected by the SLM.
sr.title: Sr

nlayers.description: Number of layers in the SLM.
nlayers.title: Nlayers

initial_channel.description: Initial channels of the SLM discriminator head.
initial_channel.title: Initial Channel

StyleTTS2TrainingConfig.description:
Training hyperparameters for StyleTTS2.

Inherits shared fields from BaseTrainingConfig: batch_size,
save_top_k_ckpts, ckpt_steps/ckpt_epochs, val_check_interval,
max_epochs/max_steps, finetune_checkpoint (→ pretrained_model),
training_filelist (→ data_params.train_data),
validation_filelist (→ data_params.val_data), logger, and data workers.

batch_size.description: The number of samples to include in each batch when training. If you are running out of memory, consider lowering your batch_size.
batch_size.title: Batch Size

save_top_k_ckpts.description: The number of checkpoints to save.
save_top_k_ckpts.title: Save Top K Ckpts

ckpt_steps.description: The interval (in steps) for saving a checkpoint. By default checkpoints are saved every epoch using the 'ckpt_epochs' hyperparameter
ckpt_steps.title: Ckpt Steps

ckpt_epochs.description: The interval (in epochs) for saving a checkpoint. You can also save checkpoints after n steps by using 'ckpt_steps'
ckpt_epochs.title: Ckpt Epochs

val_check_interval.description: How often to check the validation set. Pass a float in the range [0.0, 1.0] to check after a fraction of the training epoch. Pass an int to check after a fixed number of training batches.
val_check_interval.title: Val Check Interval

check_val_every_n_epoch.description: Run validation after every n epochs. Defaults to 1, but if you have a small dataset you should change this to be larger to speed up training
check_val_every_n_epoch.title: Check Val Every N Epoch

max_epochs.description: Stop training after this many epochs
max_epochs.title: Max Epochs

max_steps.description: Stop training after this many steps
max_steps.title: Max Steps

finetune_checkpoint.description: Automatically resume training from a checkpoint loaded from this path.
finetune_checkpoint.title: Finetune Checkpoint

training_filelist.description: The path to a filelist containing samples belonging to your training set.
training_filelist.title: Training Filelist

validation_filelist.description: The path to a filelist containing samples belonging to your validation set.
validation_filelist.title: Validation Filelist

filelist_loader.description: Advanced. The function to use to load the filelist.
filelist_loader.title: Filelist Loader

val_data_workers.description: The number of CPU workers to use when loading data during validation.
val_data_workers.title: Val Data Workers

train_data_workers.description: The number of CPU workers to use when loading data during training.
train_data_workers.title: Train Data Workers

epochs_2nd.description: Number of epochs for stage 2 (joint training).
epochs_2nd.title: Epochs 2Nd

max_len.description: Maximum clip length in Mel frames used during training. You can lower this or your batch size if you run into out-of-memory errors. Advanced: In time, this is equal to max_len * (hop_length / sampling rate). e.g. with a max_len of 800, a hop length of 300 and a sampling rate of 24kHz, this is equal to ten seconds (800 * (300 / 24000)).
max_len.title: Max Len

first_stage_path.description: Filename (relative to log_dir) where the stage 1 checkpoint is saved.
first_stage_path.title: First Stage Path

second_stage_load_pretrained.description: If True, load the stage 1 checkpoint when starting stage 2 training.
second_stage_load_pretrained.title: Second Stage Load Pretrained

load_only_params.description: If True, load only model parameters from a checkpoint (skip optimizer state and epoch number).
load_only_params.title: Load Only Params

root_path.description: Root directory that audio file paths in the filelist are relative to.
root_path.title: Root Path

ood_data.description: Path to out-of-distribution texts used for validation audio generation.
ood_data.title: Ood Data

min_length.description: Minimum text length (characters) when sampling OOD validation texts.
min_length.title: Min Length

StyleTTS2TrainingConfig.title: StyleTTS2TrainingConfig

silence.description: The symbol(s) used to indicate silence.
silence.title: Silence

punctuation.description: EveryVoice will combine punctuation and normalize it into a set of five permissible types of punctuation to help tractable training.

to_replace.description: Map of match-to-replacement to apply on training and run-time text, before cleaners are applied. Superceded by language_to_replace when processing text in a language which has language-specific text replacements, which are in turn superceded by dataset_to_replace when processing a dataset which has dataset-specific text replacements.
to_replace.title: Global text replacements

language_to_replace.description: Map from language code to text replacement maps. Supercedes the global text replacements when defined for a given language. Superceded by dataset_to_replace when processing a dataset which has dataset-specific text replacements.
language_to_replace.title: Language-specific text replacements

dataset_to_replace.description: Map from dataset label to replacement maps. Supercedes both the global text replacements and language_to_replace when defined for a given dataset.
dataset_to_replace.title: Dataset-specific text replacements.

cleaners.description: List of cleaners to apply to all datasets and run-time data. Superceded by language_cleaners when processing text in a language which has language-specific cleaners, which are in turn superceded by dataset_cleaners when processing a dataset which has dataset-specific cleaners.
cleaners.title: Global cleaners

language_cleaners.description: Map from language code to cleaner lists. Supercedes the global cleaners when defined for a given language. Superceded by dataset_cleaners when processing a dataset which has dataset-specific cleaners.
language_cleaners.title: Language-specific cleaners

dataset_cleaners.description: Map from dataset label to cleaner lists. Supercedes both the global cleaners and language_cleaners when defined for a given dataset.
dataset_cleaners.title: Dataset-specific cleaners

g2p_engines.description:
User defined or external G2P engines.
See https://github.com/EveryVoiceTTS/everyvoice_g2p_template_plugin to implement your own G2P.
g2p_engines.title: External G2P

split_text.description: Whether or not to perform text splitting (also referred to as text chunking) at inference time. Instead of synthesizing an entire utterance, the utterance will be split into smaller chunks and re-combined after synthesis. This can lead to more natural synthesis for long-form (i.e. paragraph) synthesis.
split_text.title: Split Text

boundaries.description: Strong and Weak boundaries on which text splitting is to be performed, for every language.
boundaries.title: Boundaries

contact.description: EveryVoice requires a contact name and email to help prevent misuse. Please read our Guide <https://docs.everyvoice.ca/latest/> to understand more about the importance of misuse prevention with TTS.

path_to_model_config_file.description: Path to an external model configuration file.
path_to_model_config_file.title: Path To Model Config File

path_to_training_config_file.description: Path to an external training configuration file.
path_to_training_config_file.title: Path To Training Config File

path_to_preprocessing_config_file.description: Path to an external preprocessing configuration file.
path_to_preprocessing_config_file.title: Path To Preprocessing Config File

path_to_text_config_file.description: Path to an external text configuration file.
path_to_text_config_file.title: Path To Text Config File

pretrained.description: Paths to frozen pretrained backbone models.
